channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Utah Hockey Club celebrates opening game with evening-long Delta Center plaza party
item.description: Just 172 days after it was announced Utah would be joining the National Hockey League, the Utah Hockey Club is set to play its first game in its inaugural season.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Where to watch Padres vs. Dodgers NLDS Game 3
item.description: The San Diego Padres and the Los Angeles Dodgers will meet again in Game 3 of the best-of-five National League Division Series Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Chicago Fire FC names Gregg Berhalter, former U.S. Men's National Team coach, as director of football and head coach
item.description: CHICAGO — Chicago Fire FC has hired former U.S. Men's National Soccer Team head coach Gregg Berhalter as its head coach and director of football, the Major League Soccer team announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Grimace takes the 7 train to the Mets game
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Like many other Mets fans, Grimace took the No. 7 train to the Mets game at Citi Field Tuesday. The Amazins sold out Citi Field for Game 3 of the National League Division Series against the Phillies. Given the crowd, fans were encouraged to take public transit to the game.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Jets fire head coach Robert Saleh after a 2-3 start to the season, AP source says
item.description: The New York Jets have fired head coach Robert Saleh after the team's 2-3 start in his fourth season, a person familiar with the decision told The Associated Press. Saleh was 20-36 as coach of the Jets, who are trying to snap the NFL's longest active playoff drought at 13 seasons.
item.dc:creator: DENNIS WASZAK Jr., Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Padres return to Petco Park in NLDS: What to know before you go
item.description: San Diego Padres fans are ecstatic to welcome back their team to Petco Park for games three and four of the National League Division Series against the Los Angeles Dodgers.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Taylor Swift is back to watch Travis Kelce and the Chiefs with Eras Tour soon to resume
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Taylor Swift was back at Arrowhead Stadium on Monday night to see her boyfriend, Travis Kelce, and the Kansas City Chiefs play the New Orleans Saints after the pop superstar had missed the team's previous two games on the road.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tossed balls from stands, apparently aimed at Profar, interrupt Padres' win in NLDS Game 2
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Tempers flared on the field and in the stands at Dodger Stadium, with rowdy fans tossing baseballs, apparently at San Diego left fielder Jurickson Profar, and then trash that caused a 12-minute wait between pitches during the seventh inning of the Padres' 10-2 win in NL Division S
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What is rhabdo? How intense workouts could seriously hurt you
item.description: A dozen Tufts University men’s lacrosse players were diagnosed with the rare muscle injury in September.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Karl-Anthony Towns scores 10 points in abbreviated preseason debut with New York Knicks
item.description: Jalen Brunson thought twice about going 1-on-2 in transition, instead dribbled back outside and flipped the ball behind him to Karl-Anthony Towns, who made a 3-pointer with a defender in his face.
item.dc:creator: STEVE REED, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: PHOTOS: Padres look to rebound in game 2 of NLDS against Dodgers
item.description: Photos: The San Diego Padres are facing off against the Los Angeles Dodgers in Game 2 of the NL Division Series on Sunday, with Yu Darvish starting for the Padres.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: Caleb Williams, Bears offense overwhelm Panthers, advance to 3-2
item.description: CHICAGO — Caleb Williams continued his improvement with a breakout game Sunday against the Carolina Panthers, as the Chicago Bears used a first-half offensive blitz to cruise to an easy victory Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Baseball fans discuss Dodgers’ chances in NLDS Game 2 over donuts
item.description: After a rollercoaster of emotions in Game 1 of the National League Divisional Series, baseball lovers were up early getting donuts in Inglewood and eager to share their thoughts with the news on Sunday's matchup between the Los Angeles Dodgers and the San Diego Padres.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Washington pulls away in 4th quarter to beat No. 10 Michigan 27-17 in title game rematch
item.description: Washington quarterback Will Rogers watched the final seconds tick away and wondered if the field at Husky Stadium was about to be flooded with purple.
item.dc:creator: TIM BOOTH, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lane Thomas hits 3-run homer, Cleveland's bullpen torments Tigers as Guardians win ALDS opener 7-0
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Lane Thomas hit a three-run homer in a five-run outburst before Detroit got an out, and the Cleveland Guardians unleashed their lights-out bullpen to complete a four-hitter in a 7-0 win over the Tigers in an AL Division Series opener on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Vanderbilt takes down No. 1 Alabama 40-35 in historic college football victory
item.description: Fans of the Vanderbilt Commodores danced onto the field, jumping up and down as they tore down a goal post, carried it out of the stadium and rang out sweet sounds of victory.
item.dc:creator: TERESA M. WALKER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Clayton Kershaw won't pitch for the Dodgers in playoffs after big toe injury fails to heal
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Clayton Kershaw, sidelined since late August with a toe injury, won’t pitch again this season for the Los Angeles Dodgers.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dodgers' Kershaw, Guardians' Lively, Tigers' Mize and Yankees' Stroman are left off ALDS rosters
item.description: Pitchers Clayton Kershaw and Joe Kelly were left off the Los Angeles Dodgers' roster for their NL Division Series against the San Diego Padres that started Saturday. San Diego added right-handers Martín Pérez and Alek Jacob, who were skipped from the Wild Card Series roster against Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jazz counting on Hendricks and George to take big strides
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - With its commitment to a youth movement, the Utah Jazz are counting on Keyonte George and Taylor Hendricks to both take big strides in their second seasons this year. After playing in 75 games as a rookie, George wore down a bit at the end of the year.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Bowles says the Buccaneers will learn from their mistakes in overtime loss to division-rival Falcons
item.description: The Tampa Bay Buccaneers have no one to blame but themselves for failing to take advantage of an opportunity to tighten their grip on first place in the NFC South.
item.dc:creator: FRED GOODALL, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Is the Chicago Bears run game back?
item.description: The Chicago Bears are relying on their running game and short passing game to replicate their Week 4 performance against the Carolina Panthers, as they have shifted away from a run-dominant offense under their new offensive coordinator.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Leading in a new league: Oceanside golfer wins top spot in nine matches
item.description: “Medalist of the match is basically between the two teams, the individual who has the lowest score,” explained Oceanside High School golfer Kassidy Stevens.
item.dc:creator: Frank Jefferson, Fox 5

item.title: Shaboozey to perform prior to Utah Hockey Club's first home game
item.description: The National Hockey League has announced that the creator of the smash hit "A Bar Song (Tipsy)," Shaboozey will perform outside of the Delta Center ahead of the Utah Hockey Club's first regular season game.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, ABC 4
